services: EGL AI Sales Assistant (client growth system)
Lead Generation (also serves auto body, diesel, mobile mechanics)
typical_monthly_usd: $1,500-$2,500/mo retainer (excluding ad spend) for a single-location independent shop; $950-$2,500/mo range reported on their site for management fee alone
setup_fee_usd: Not published [uncertain] — no setup fee mentioned in any tier on G2 pricing page
min_ad_spend_usd: ~$1,000/mo per service bay recommended (e.g. 5-bay shop = ~$5,000/mo Google Ads budget); total $1,500-$3,500/mo packages typically include ad spend at the low end
contract_length_months: month-to-month / no long-term contract (per agency claim: 'clients stay because of results, not because they're locked in')
cancellation_policy: No long-term lock-in reported; exact notice period not published [uncertain]
asset_ownership: Shop retains website, domain, Google Ads account, GBP, and creative [uncertain — not explicitly published, but consistent with their 'no lock-in' positioning and standard for transparent indie agencies]
pricing_transparency: Tiered / partially published — starting price ($590) listed on G2; tier ranges discussed on their own site and podcast; specific tier feature breakdowns require a sales call
known_pain_points: Limited third-party review volume — Clutch profile had no reviews at last capture, making independent verification of outcomes thin
Smaller agency footprint — capacity questions if Koffman wanted multi-location / enterprise-grade SLAs [uncertain]
Primarily mechanical-AR oriented; collision capability exists (lead-gen page for auto body shops) but is not their core competency
notable_clients: Client list not publicly enumerated [uncertain] — published case studies and podcast guests reference independent AR shop owners, but no named MSO or franchise client roster
pros: Founders Brian & Kim Walker previously owned a $2M auto repair shop — operator empathy is genuine and shows in messaging
Transparent-ish pricing vs Hibu/Turnkey — published starting price + ranges, ROI calculator, no contract lock-in
Niche focus on independent AR shops — content, ad copy, and tracking tuned to repair-shop KPIs (cost-per-repair-job, booked appointments)
Active thought leadership (Auto Repair Marketing Podcast, blog with 23+ industry guides)
Proprietary EGL AI Sales Assistant included in some tiers
Shop typically owns the website, ad account, and creative (consistent with their no-lock-in stance)
cons: AR-first — not a collision-repair specialist; would not replace BodyShop Booster for CR workflow
Thin third-party review profile (Clutch/G2 have minimal independent reviews); reputation rests on owner-published content
Small agency — bus-factor / capacity risk for a larger operator like Koffman
Lower price tier ($590) is likely website-only or very light Google Ads management; real comparable scope to Hibu/Turnkey lives at $1,500-$3,500/mo
koffman_relevance: MEDIUM — strong potential replacement for Turnkey Marketing on the mechanical-AR side (similar scope: website + Google Ads + GBP + analytics) at comparable or lower cost, with no contract lock-in and shop-owned assets. Would NOT cover Koffman's CR department — pair with BodyShop Booster or another collision specialist for that. Element DMA does have an auto-body lead-gen offering, but their published case studies and founder background are mechanical-AR. Best framed as: 'Element DMA replaces Turnkey for AR; BodyShop Booster bolts onto the CR dept; Hibu can be cut entirely.'
